title: Malformed base64 could be reported as a saved artifact

evidence.quote: throw new Error('The artifact contains invalid base64 data')
evidence.note: Repair boundary added after the audit found direct permissive Buffer.from decoding.

observed: Malformed base64 could decode permissively, be written, and return success.
expected_boundary: Invalid encoded content aborts before a target file is written.
failure_mechanism: The save helper trusted Buffer.from base64 decoding without validating alphabet, padding, or round-trip integrity.
break_it_angle: Save not!base64 as a generated image and inspect the filesystem.
impact: A generated document or image could be silently corrupted while the UI reported success.

mitigation.minimal_repair: Validate base64 and decode/re-encode equivalence before writing.
mitigation.local_guardrail: Leave no target file on validation failure.
mitigation.behavior_test: Malformed base64 rejects and the target remains absent.

implementation_assessment.rationale: One main-process helper and filesystem regression close the boundary.

non_goals: inspect or transcode valid image/document bytes

confidence_calibration.rationale: Permissive decode followed by unconditional write/success was a deterministic code path.

skill_escalation.reason: The corrupt write previously produced a success indication.

title: Concurrent downloads could reserve the same filename

evidence.note: Reservation added after the audit found filesystem-only check-then-act selection.

observed: Two will-download events could both observe the same path as absent before either file existed.
expected_boundary: In-flight destinations participate in collision selection.
failure_mechanism: The uniqueness check considered disk state but not concurrent reservations.
break_it_angle: Start two downloads named brief.pdf in the same event turn.
impact: Downloads could contend for or overwrite one workspace artifact path.

mitigation.minimal_repair: Reserve the selected path until Electron reports the download done.
mitigation.local_guardrail: Include reservations and existing files in the same name predicate.
mitigation.behavior_test: Two simultaneous names resolve to base and numbered paths.

implementation_assessment.rationale: The coordination is process-local to one Electron session and requires a focused event regression.

non_goals: coordinate downloads across separate Gosling processes

confidence_calibration.rationale: The two event callbacks deterministically shared a filesystem-only availability predicate.

skill_escalation.reason: The defect is a check-then-act collision between in-flight events.

title: Unroutable downloads silently fell back outside the workspace

evidence.quote: could not use a workspace output folder
evidence.note: Typed feedback added after the audit found silent returns in will-download.

observed: A missing or rejected routing config returned without setting a path or notifying the renderer.
expected_boundary: If a download cannot honor the workspace, the operator is told and given a recovery action.
failure_mechanism: The main-process listener treated absence of a route as a neutral no-op while Electron continued its default behavior.
break_it_angle: Make the configured output inaccessible and initiate a native download.
impact: The user searches the workspace while the artifact lands elsewhere.

mitigation.minimal_repair: Emit an event from every unrouted branch and show a relink warning.
mitigation.local_guardrail: Do not claim or toast workspace success for an unrouted download.
mitigation.behavior_test: Missing config invokes the warning callback with the filename.

implementation_assessment.rationale: One typed IPC event connects the already-known main failure to actionable renderer feedback.

confidence_calibration.rationale: The no-config branch deterministically returned with no renderer signal.

skill_escalation.reason: The silent status mismatch changed the actual destination.

title: In-memory artifact saves lacked a resource bound

evidence.quote: The in-memory artifact is too large to save safely
evidence.note: Bound added after the audit found unbounded renderer content and base64 allocation.

observed: The save helper accepted an arbitrary-length content string and could allocate decoded bytes in the main process.
expected_boundary: In-memory artifact payloads have an explicit bound; large existing files use streaming copy.
failure_mechanism: Neither the renderer save seam nor the main helper capped content length.
break_it_angle: Submit a very large embedded tool result and observe main-process memory pressure.
impact: Desktop responsiveness or availability could degrade; runtime exhaustion was not reproduced.

mitigation.minimal_repair: Reject in-memory content above 256 MiB before opening the dialog.
mitigation.local_guardrail: Keep file-source copies on the filesystem path rather than materializing preview bytes.
mitigation.behavior_test: Injected small cap rejects before destination selection.

implementation_assessment.rationale: A centralized size policy covers every content save without changing file-copy behavior.

confidence_calibration.rationale: The missing bound was static; actual OOM or stall behavior was not reproduced.

skill_escalation.reason: The consequence is main-process resource pressure.

title: Unicode-heavy artifact names could exceed filesystem byte limits

evidence.note: UTF-8 byte truncation added after the audit found a JavaScript character-count cap at the portable filename boundary.

observed: A multi-byte Unicode filename could remain under 180 JavaScript characters while exceeding a common filesystem component byte limit.
expected_boundary: Every proposed leaf name fits the router's UTF-8 byte budget without splitting a code point or losing its bounded extension.
failure_mechanism: The sanitizer measured UTF-16 string length instead of encoded filesystem bytes.
break_it_angle: Route an 80-emoji PNG title and measure the proposed leaf in UTF-8 bytes.
impact: A generated artifact save or native download could fail despite a syntactically valid title.

mitigation.minimal_repair: Truncate shared portable filenames to 180 UTF-8 bytes and reserve a bounded extension budget.
mitigation.local_guardrail: Apply the sanitizer before both explicit default paths and native download destinations.
mitigation.behavior_test: An 80-emoji PNG name is at most 180 bytes, retains .png, and contains no replacement character.

implementation_assessment.rationale: One pure shared sanitizer and boundary regression cover all routed artifact names.

confidence_calibration.rationale: The over-budget encoded output was deterministic; native rejection was not exercised on every supported filesystem.

skill_escalation.reason: The primary consequence is a failed save/download for a valid artifact.

title: Older async validation could restore a stale workspace download route

evidence.note: Revision guard added after the audit found awaited validators writing a shared map without ordering protection.

observed: An earlier routing update could finish validation after a newer update and overwrite the newer per-window config; cleanup could likewise be undone by an in-flight validator.
expected_boundary: Only the most recently received config or clear operation may publish routing state.
failure_mechanism: Async filesystem validation separated request ordering from the final shared-map write, with no revision check around the await.
break_it_angle: Hold validation for workspace A, publish validated workspace B, then release A; also clear the window before releasing a pending validator.
impact: A native download could be routed into the previously active workspace output folder.

mitigation.minimal_repair: Assign a per-window revision before validation and commit only when it remains current.
mitigation.local_guardrail: Clearing a window also advances its revision so pending validation cannot restore state.
mitigation.behavior_test: Forced out-of-order validations retain the newer config and clear-during-validation remains empty.

implementation_assessment.rationale: A focused registry makes publication ordering explicit without changing renderer or Electron Session contracts.

non_goals: cancel filesystem validation already in progress

confidence_calibration.rationale: The unguarded stale-write mechanism was source-confirmed and forced-interleave tested after repair; a packaged Electron misroute was not observed.

skill_escalation.reason: The stale routing state changes the native download destination.
